extraction.one_line_summary: MILAN Jonathan won his Grand Tour debut sprint in San Salvo Marina after a crash at 3.7km split the field just outside the 3km safety rule
tactical_patterns: Crash at 3.7km, only 700m before 3km rule protection, caught GC riders including McNulty/Vine who lost positions
LAPEYRA Paul dominated intermediate KOM sprints on debut, shedding VERRE Alessandro deliberately to secure jersey before home region stage
Milan sprinted through center rather than following leadout trains, suggesting better positioning awareness than established sprinters

rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: positive: Track power translated to road sprint victory on Grand Tour debut, beating experienced sprinters with centerline positioning
rider_signals.LAPEYRA Paul: positive: Controlled KOM competition tactically on first GT road stage, took jersey at 22 years old
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Caught in late crash, failed to navigate final 4km despite winning in Teramo previously in 2011
rider_signals.POGACAR Tadej: positive: Avoided 3.7km crash with Roglic/Thomas, showing positioning discipline even on easy sprint stage
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: Lost GC positions from 4th to outside top-7 by being caught in crash beyond 3km protection
rider_signals.VINE Jay: negative: Also caught behind crash, dropped GC places despite easy stage profile favoring position conservation
